meaning: n.临日本海之西伯利亚的不冻港
examples: Visit the museum and you will know many new things about the history of Nakhodka.
Nakhodka, which actually means "discovery", was discovered by the Russian sailing ship America in 1859.
In 2010 the museum of Nakhodka presented its third project called museum on Rollers that won another grant of a Russian charitable fund.
Set just east of Nakhodka at the Pacific terminus of the Trans-Siberian railway, Vostochny is the largest and probably the best managed port in the Russian Far East.
And though Japan's government is urging Russia to take a more expensive route northwards and to Nakhodka, Russia's closest big port to Japan, this would run similar risks.
